title: valeriana pratensis subsp. pratensis
intro: Valeriana pratensis subsp. pratensis, also known as meadow valerian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae, characterized by its fragrant flowers and leaves that are rich in valerenic acid. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include sedative and antispasmodic effects, making it a natural remedy for anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The health benefits of meadow valerian include improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and relief from menstrual cramps and other muscular spasms. Meadow valerian can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
